citations.document_title: Employment Lands Study Planning Proposal - July 2025
citations.claim: This planning proposal aims to update Hornsby Council's planning rules to boost economic activity and support future development of job-generating land across Hornsby Shire .
citations.fact_notes: No verifiable facts extracted from claim

citations.document_title: Employment Lands Study Planning Proposal - July 2025
citations.claim: Specifically, the proposal will allow "light industries" in E1 Local Centre zones, but not in those areas identified as "Area 1" on the Land Zoning Map .

citations.document_title: Employment Lands Study Planning Proposal - July 2025
citations.claim: This means light industries will be permitted in larger local centres that were previously zoned B2 Local Centre .

citations.document_title: Employment Lands Study Planning Proposal - July 2025
citations.claim: This is intended to increase the diversity of jobs and opportunities in accessible locations .
citations.fact_notes: No verifiable facts extracted from claim

citations.document_title: Employment Lands Study Planning Proposal - July 2025
citations.claim: Light industries will not be permitted in smaller centres that were formerly zoned B1 Neighbourhood Centre, which are now identified as E1 Local Centre (Area 1) .

citations.document_title: Hornsby Employment Lands Study - May 2021
citations.claim: Light industries will not be permitted in smaller centres that were formerly zoned B1 Neighbourhood Centre, which are now identified as E1 Local Centre (Area 1) .

citations.document_title: Employment Lands Study Planning Proposal - July 2025
citations.claim: For local centres, new rules will require active street frontages in the E1 Local Centre Zone .

citations.document_title: Planning Proposal ELS
citations.claim: For local centres, new rules will require active street frontages in the E1 Local Centre Zone .

citations.document_title: Employment Lands Study Planning Proposal - July 2025
citations.claim: The goal is to ensure businesses at street level attract pedestrian traffic and support the vitality of local centres , helping to create vibrant and safe places .
citations.fact_notes: No verifiable facts extracted from claim

citations.document_title: Planning Proposal ELS
citations.claim: The goal is to ensure businesses at street level attract pedestrian traffic and support the vitality of local centres , helping to create vibrant and safe places .

citations.document_title: Planning Proposal ELS
citations.claim: The goal is to ensure businesses at street level attract pedestrian traffic and support the vitality of local centres , helping to create vibrant and safe places .

citations.document_title: Employment Lands Study Planning Proposal - July 2025
citations.claim: The proposal also seeks to make industrial land more appealing for investment by allowing greater flexibility in new industrial designs and protecting these areas from other types of development .
citations.fact_notes: No verifiable facts extracted from claim

citations.document_title: Employment Lands Study Planning Proposal - July 2025
citations.claim: There are no changes proposed for how flood-prone land is managed; existing rules will continue to apply .
citations.fact_notes: No verifiable facts extracted from claim

citations.document_title: Planning Proposal ELS - RFS Consultation
citations.claim: For land zoned E1 Local Centre, new developments will need to include active shop fronts facing the street .

citations.document_title: Planning Proposal ELS - RFS Consultation
citations.claim: Also, some light industries will now be allowed in the E1 Local Centre zone, but not in areas identified as “Area 1” on the Land Zoning Map .

citations.document_title: Planning Proposal ELS - RFS Consultation
citations.claim: Serviced apartments will no longer count towards the ‘non-residential’ floorspace calculations .
citations.fact_notes: No verifiable facts extracted from claim

citations.document_title: Planning Proposal ELS - RFS Consultation
citations.claim: The maximum size for new food and drink businesses will be limited to 150 square metres of gross floor area .

citations.document_title: Planning Proposal ELS - RFS Consultation
citations.claim: Building height limits could increase up to a maximum of 20 metres in some of these industrial areas .

citations.document_title: Employment Lands Study Planning Proposal - July 2025
citations.claim: Building height limits could increase up to a maximum of 20 metres in some of these industrial areas .
citations.fact_notes: The claim states the increase applies 'in some of these industrial areas', which is directly supported by the chunk's statement 'Increase the height of building control up to a maximum of 20 metres in some E4 General Industrial zoned precincts'.

citations.document_title: Draft amendments to the HDCP 2024 - July 2025
citations.claim: Current limits in E4 General Industrial areas are 10.5m (Area K) or 14.5m (Area N) .

citations.document_title: Planning Proposal ELS - RFS Consultation
citations.claim: Specifically for the Dural Industrial Precinct, the maximum Floor Space Ratio (FSR) is proposed to increase from the current 0.7:1 to 1:1 .

citations.document_title: Employment Lands Study Planning Proposal - July 2025
citations.claim: Specifically for the Dural Industrial Precinct, the maximum Floor Space Ratio (FSR) is proposed to increase from the current 0.7:1 to 1:1 .

citations.document_title: Employment Lands Study Planning Proposal - July 2025
citations.claim: This change aims to align Dural with other E4 General Industrial areas like Mount Kuring-Gai, Thornleigh, and Asquith, which already have a 1:1 FSR .
